user.title: Particle Man
user.description: Enter your Google Maps API key and location. Additional options (forecast days, pollen, local AQI) can be configured after setup via the Configure button.

error.invalid_auth: Invalid API key or insufficient permissions
error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to Google Air Quality API

data_description.latitude: Latitude of the location to monitor.
data_description.longitude: Longitude of the location to monitor.
data_description.update_interval: How often to poll both APIs (15–1440 minutes).

data.language_code: Language code (BCP-47, e.g. en, es, fr)

data_description.forecast_days: Number of daily forecast entries on all sensors (1 = today only, 5 = maximum).
data_description.language_code: Language for display names and health recommendations.

data_description.enable_local_aqi: Adds a sensor for the regional AQI standard (e.g. US AQI for US locations).
data_description.local_aqi_code: Preferred local AQI standard when overriding the regional default.
data_description.include_health_recommendations: Adds per-population-group health guidance as an attribute on the AQI and pollen type sensors.

data_description.include_plant_sensors: Creates a sensor for each individual plant species (e.g. Oak, Ragweed). Adds ~10–15 entities per location.
data_description.include_plant_descriptions: Adds family, genus, seasonality, and cross-reaction attributes to plant sensors.

data.aq_monthly_limit: Air Quality API monthly call limit
data.enforce_limits: Enforce limits (suspend polling when limit is reached)

data_description.aq_monthly_limit: Monthly Air Quality API call limit for usage tracking. Defaults to the Google free tier (10,000 calls/month). Set to 0 to disable warnings.
data_description.pollen_monthly_limit: Monthly Pollen API call limit for usage tracking. Defaults to the Google free tier (5,000 calls/month). Set to 0 to disable warnings.
data_description.reset_day: Day of the month your billing period resets (1–28). Defaults to the 1st.
data_description.enforce_limits: When enabled, polling stops when either API limit is reached. When disabled, limits are tracked but not enforced.
